title: Release Reconciliation, Incident Response, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A joint reader, publisher, accessibility, security, privacy, database, operations, and support board. The board needs source-bound artifacts, migration and container evidence, publisher and reader SLOs, capacity findings, security controls, rehearsed backup and restore, incident containment, rollback, and owned residual risks. Planned artifact: Deliver Go Feed Service Release and Disaster Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners…
objectives: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.
Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.
Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 3589 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend source-to-artifact provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risk; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Capacity Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against cpu, memory, connections, locks, and budgets. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Capacity Model, keep Feed Go Capacity Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. options.text: A handler requests-per-second benchmark proves ingestion capacity. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Feed Go Capacity Model.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Go Capacity Model for defensible production Go feed aggregator.
State the Feed Go Capacity Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
